Test Automation

Playwright Chronicles: Crafting Elegant Automation in JS/TS from Scratch — Part 33: Building a Smarter Test Base by Custom Naming and File Upload Automation

4 min read
Just now

--

In the 32nd edition of this series (Playwright Chronicles: Crafting Elegant Automation in JS/TS from Scratch — Part 32: Turning Test Data into Playwright Fixtures | by Suresh Madhusanka Rodrigo | AI & QA Nexus | Jul, 2026 | Medium), we demonstrated how to create a fixture for our test data related to the Web Tables page and how to use that fixture data effectively inside automation scripts. That laid the foundation for building cleaner, more modular Playwright tests.
In this edition, we will extend that knowledge further by focusing on two key areas:
- Custom naming for extended test: Showing how we can give a different name (like testBase) instead of always using the standardized test.
- Extending fixtures for file upload/download: Defining new fixture data for file upload scenarios and writing scripts that consume this data to validate upload and download functionality.
Exporting the Extended Fixture with a Different Name
In the 32nd edition of blog post, we imported the extended fixture using the standardized name test. However, Playwright allows…
